summary: Testing of the new Product Categories management feature (backend CRUD + admin UI + public filters). Backend: added TestCategories class (8 tests) to /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. All targeted category flows pass — public GET returns 6 seeded slugs sorted by order; auth-gated POST/PUT/DELETE work; slug='all' rejected 400; duplicate slug 409; delete blocked with clear French message when products attached (5 engrais products); update propagates slug change to products.category; slug conflict on update returns 409. Frontend: /admin/categories renders 6 rows, add-btn opens dialog, create TEST_Semences appears in table, edit updates the name, delete of 'Engrais' (with products) is blocked with the exact backend toast 'Suppression impossible : 5 produit(s) sont rattaché(s)…' and row remains, delete of TEST_Semences_Edited succeeds. /produits filter chips are dynamic (filter-all + filter-engrais/fertilisants/herbicides/insecticides/fongicides/equipements) and clicking filter-engrais shows exactly the 5 engrais products with human badge 'Engrais'. Regression: all previous 24 backend tests still green.

minor.issue: Empty/whitespace-only slug is NOT rejected with 400 as specified. slugify() falls back to a random uuid[:8] so `value='   '` silently creates a category with a nonsensical slug (e.g. `175a40de`). Spec explicitly asks for 400 'Slug de catégorie invalide.'. Guard should reject before slugify falls back, or validate name is non-empty. Test marked xfail in TestCategories.test_create_empty_slug.

ui_bugs.issue: data-testid for Catégories link is `admin-nav-cat-gories` (é replaced by '-') instead of the spec's `admin-nav-categories`. toTestId helper in AdminLayout.jsx line 46 uses `n.label.toLowerCase().replace(/\s|é/g, '-')` — same accent-stripping bug flagged in iter2 for Header.jsx. Recommend `normalize('NFD').replace(/\p{Diacritic}/gu, '')` then `.replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g,'-')`.

action_items: Backend (minor): Validate empty/whitespace slug in _normalize_cat_value BEFORE slugify() falls back to uuid, or make slugify raise for empty input in this context. Currently POST with value='   ' returns 200 with random uuid slug instead of 400.

Frontend (cosmetic): Fix AdminLayout.jsx line 46 toTestId regex so accents are properly stripped — expected testid `admin-nav-categories`, currently emits `admin-nav-cat-gories`. Same class of bug as iter2 Header.jsx toTestId.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py slugify() returns `new_id()[:8]` fallback for empty strings — masks invalid input in category creation. Consider a stricter slugify_strict() variant that raises for empty results, or explicit empty-check in _normalize_cat_value before calling slugify.

PUT /api/admin/categories cascades products.category on slug rename (good) but there is no similar transactional guarantee — if update fails mid-way (very unlikely with two ops here) products could reference the new slug while the category row was not persisted. Acceptable for this scale, worth noting.
Category order is not unique — two categories could share the same order integer; sorting then falls back to name. Fine for now.
AdminLayout.jsx toTestId: same accent handling bug carried over from iter2 (only strips é, not è/à/ï/ô etc).

success_rate.backend: 97% (31/32 targeted; 1 xfail on empty-slug spec deviation)
success_rate.frontend: 100% of requested flows verified (admin CRUD, blocked delete toast, public filters, category badge)

seed_data_creation: Only ephemeral TEST_-prefixed categories/products created during pytest + one 'TEST_Semences_Edited' in the browser flow; all cleaned up. Verified /api/categories at end returns exactly the 6 seeded slugs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Categories: sidebar link testid is admin-nav-cat-gories (é→-) NOT admin-nav-categories — click via a[href='/admin/categories'] as a safer selector. Save button inside the CrudManager dialog is button[type='submit'] (no dedicated testid). The public filter chips DO expose stable data-testid=filter-<slug>. Delete uses native window.confirm(); register `page.on('dialog', d: d.accept())` BEFORE clicking. Backend seeded categories collection is now idempotent (seeds only if empty), verified via the full CRUD cycle test.
